# Test: Existing PR Update (Edge Case)
# Validates that releasing when a PR already exists handles the situation correctly.
#
# Prerequisites: k3d and Gitea running (handled by TestE2E)
# Environment: GITEA_URL, GITEA_TOKEN, E2E_CLUSTER are injected by test runner

# Step 1: Create app repository
mkdir app
cd app
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --repo=test-app --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/existing/v1
exists apx.yaml

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Initial schema v1.0.0'
exec git tag proto/existing/v1/v1.0.0

# Step 2: First release attempt (dry-run to verify command works)
exec apx release prepare --module-path=internal/apis/proto/existing/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/existing-test --dry-run
stdout 'Dry-run mode'

# Step 3: Make a change and release again (simulate existing PR scenario)
exec sh -c 'echo "// Updated field" >> internal/apis/proto/existing/v1/existing.proto'
exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Update schema v1.0.1'
exec git tag proto/existing/v1/v1.0.1

exec apx release prepare --module-path=internal/apis/proto/existing/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/existing-test --version=v1.0.1 --dry-run
stdout 'Dry-run mode'
stdout 'v1.0.1'

# Step 4: Verify git state is clean
exec git status --porcelain
! stdout '.'

cd ..

# Success: Existing PR handling validated
# - Multiple releases of same module succeed in dry-run
# - Version progression works correctly
# - Git state remains clean after release operations
